title: A wedge for a lead-through system
abstract: The present invention concerns a wedge for a lead-through system. The wedge comprises a first wedge element (1), a second wedge element (2), a third wedge element (3) and a fourth wedge element (4). The first and second wedge elements (1, 2) are arranged moveable towards and away from each other. The third and fourth wedge elements (3, 4) are placed on opposite sides of the first and second wedge elements (1, 2), abutting the first and second wedge elements (1, 2) along sloping surfaces. The wedge elements (1, 2, 3, 4) and the sloping surfaces are so arranged that the third and fourth wedge elements (3, 4) will be moved away from each other when the first and second wedge elements (1,2) are moved toward each other and that the third and fourth wedge elements (3, 4) are moved toward each other when the first and second wedge elements (1, 2) are moved away from each other. The wedge further comprises a bracket (5) on the outside of the first wedge element (1). The bracket (5) receives a socket (6) arranged to be able to rotate in relation to the bracket (5). A compression screw (7) is connected with one end to the second wedge element (2) and goes through an opening of the first wedge element (1). A head (10) of the compression screw (7) is received inside the socket (6) of the bracket (5).
claims:
Claims 1. A wedge for a lead-through system, which wedge comprises a first wedge element (1), a second wedge element (2), a third wedge element (3) and a fourth wedge element (4), whereby the first and second wedge elements (1, 2) are arranged moveable towards and away from each other, whereby the third and fourth wedge elements (3, 4) are placed on opposite sides of the first and second wedge elements (1, 2) and abutting the first and second wedge elements (1, 2) along sloping surfaces, and whereby the wedge elements (1, 2, 3, 4) and the sloping surfaces are so arranged that the third and fourth wedge elements (3, 4) will be moved away from each other when the first and second wedge elements (1,2) are moved toward each other and that the third and fourth wedge elements (3, 4) are moved toward each other when the first and second wedge elements (1, 2) are moved away from each other, characterized in that it comprises a bracket (5) on the outside of the first wedge element (1), which bracket (5) receives a socket (6) arranged to be able to rotate in relation to the bracket (5), that a compression screw (7) is connected with one end to the second wedge element (2) and goes through an opening of the first wedge element (1) and that a head (10) of the compression screw (7) is received inside the socket (6) of the bracket (5). 2. The wedge of claim 1, wherein the socket (6) is locked from longitudinal movement in relation to the bracket (5). 3. The wedge of claim 1 or 2, wherein the head (10) of the compression screw (7) is hindered from rotating in relation to the socket (6) but is free to move longitudinally in relation to the socket (6). 4. The wedge of any of the previous claims, wherein the socket (6) has a cylindrical part (15) at one end, a head (17) at the opposite end and an outer circumferential groove (16) placed between the cylindrical part (15) and the head (17) and wherein the socket (6) has an inner through opening. 5. The wedge of claim 4, wherein the through opening of the socket (6) in the area of the head (17) of the socket (6) has an inner form adapted to the form of the head (10) of the compression screw (7). 6. The wedge of claim 5, wherein the through opening has a hexagonal form in the area of the head (17) of the socket (6), corresponding with a hexagonal form of the head (10) of the compression screw (7). 7. The wedge of claim 6, wherein the inner through opening of the socket (6) has a circular cross section form in the area of the cylindrical part (15) and the groove (16) of the socket (6) giving an edge inside the through opening between the part having 

 a circular inner cross section form and the part having a hexagonal inner cross section form. 8. The wedge of claim 4, wherein the bracket (5) has a detachable part (14), which detachable part (14) may be slid into place in or out of the rest of the bracket (14) by means of co-operating grooves and protruding parts, wherein the bracket (5) together with the detachable part (14) forms a circumferential ring proj ecting inwardly, wherein the socket (6) is placed in the bracket (5) and wherein the circumferential ring proj ects into the outer circumferential groove (16) of the socket (6). 9. The wedge of any of the previous claims, wherein a first sleeve (8) is received in the first wedge element (1) and a second sleeve (9) is received in the second wedge element (10), wherein the compression screw (7) have threads with opposite pitch, each co-operating with inner threads of one of the sleeves (8, 9) in such a way that depending on the direction of rotation of the compression screw (7) the first and second wedge elements (1 , 2) are moved towards or away from each other. 10. The wedge of claim 9, wherein a front fitting (11) is received in the first wedge element (1) and a rear fitting (12) is received in the second wedge element (2), wherein the first and second sleeves (8, 9) are fastened to the front fitting and rear fitting (11 , 12), respectively. 1 1. The wedge of claim 10, wherein the bracket (5) is fixed to the front fitting (11) by means of screws (13), snap-in fasteners or an adhesive. 12. The wedge of any of the claim 4-1 1, wherein in a full compressing state of the wedge, the outer end of the head (10) of the compression screw (7) is flush with the outer end of the head (17) of the socket (6). 13. The wedge of any of the previous claims, wherein the wedge is placed inside a frame of a lead-through system together with modules and stay plates.
